text.title: CI and Docker Integration
text.summary: GitHub Actions workflow with pnpm cache and Dockerfile with pnpm store mount.

section.title: GitHub Actions

rule.statement: Use `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in CI. Cache the pnpm store keyed by `pnpm-lock.yaml` hash.
rule.rationale: `--frozen-lockfile` fails if the lockfile is out of sync, catching accidental dependency mutations. Caching the store drastically reduces install time.

section.reference: GitHub Actions workflow with pnpm cache

example.code:
FROM node:20-slim AS base ENV PNPM_HOME="/pnpm" ENV PATH="$PNPM_HOME:$PATH" RUN corepack enable
FROM base AS deps WORKDIR /app COPY pnpm-lock.yaml ./ RUN --mount=type=cache,id=pnpm,target=/pnpm/store pnpm fetch
FROM deps AS build COPY . . RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --offline RUN pnpm build
FROM base AS runtime WORKDIR /app COPY --from=build /app/dist ./dist COPY --from=build /app/node_modules ./node_modules CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]
example.note: `pnpm fetch` pre-populates the store; `--offline` install uses it without network. Mount-cache persists across builds.
